method_note: Each absence row is a review question per case; cross-case patterns are not confirmed findings.

items.displacement_mechanism: Political slavery is foregrounded as metaphor while chattel slavery is structurally absent from the rights claim.
items.claim_boundary: A structural absence in the founding documents; not a peripheral oversight.

items.displacement_mechanism: Universal founding-sacrifice language naturalizes an exclusive beneficiary class.
items.claim_boundary: Review question for fuller historical corroboration.

items.metaphor_system: The people / republic as sacred political object
items.displacement_mechanism: Universal-people rhetoric suppresses the particularity of its exclusions.
items.claim_boundary: Structural absence at the founding; well-documented in historical scholarship.

items.metaphor_system: Jew / enemy as parasite / disease / poison
excluded_agents: Jewish political, cultural, and social actors
targets of Nazi persecution with no counter-voice in this corpus
items.displacement_mechanism: Pathogen framing suppresses Jewish subjectivity entirely; the ideology constructs its own victims as its agents.
items.claim_boundary: Not an absence in the usual sense — an active erasure that is the ideological goal of the texts under analysis.

excluded_agents: populations killed by the state the movement created
items.displacement_mechanism: Martyrology concentrates sacrificial meaning on perpetrators while suppressing the deaths it caused.
items.claim_boundary: Requires historical corroboration for the full scope of victim suppression.

excluded_agents: Jewish, Roma, disabled, homosexual, and other excluded populations
items.displacement_mechanism: Racial-body rhetoric naturalizes exclusion and murder as organic necessity.
items.claim_boundary: The logic of racial-body rhetoric directly authorized genocide; this is not a hypothesis but a historical fact.

items.displacement_mechanism: Bodily loss is displaced into national obligation and cemetery consecration.
items.claim_boundary: Systematic absence candidate; requires fuller corpus review before becoming a case-level finding.

items.displacement_mechanism: Providential grammar can widen guilt but also blur differentiated human agency.
items.claim_boundary: Interpretive caution, not a claim that Lincoln intentionally suppressed agency.

items.metaphor_system: Nation or government as living organism
items.displacement_mechanism: Conflict is condensed into organism survival rather than social plurality.
items.claim_boundary: Useful for rhetorical analysis; not by itself evidence of coercive policy.

excluded_agents: enemy dead and wounded as subjects
items.displacement_mechanism: Glory and destiny convert individual deaths into abstract historical capital.
items.claim_boundary: Most clearly evidenced at Eylau; requires corroboration from non-bulletin sources.

items.metaphor_system: Enemy as obstacle to order / destiny
items.displacement_mechanism: Obstacle framing suppresses enemy agency and civilian experience of conquest.
items.claim_boundary: A structural feature of the bulletin genre; requires non-French sources for the other side.

items.metaphor_system: Emperor as embodiment of France / history
items.displacement_mechanism: Embodiment rhetoric naturalizes coercive military service as voluntary devotion.
items.claim_boundary: Requires corroboration from non-official sources (memoirs, administrative records).
